blockers.title: No-JS path promises an unaudited email consent bypass
blockers.evidence: Spec AC2 requires guardian consent by explicit button press/mutation, and AC7 keeps extra consent flows out of scope. `src/pages/guardian-confirm.astro:28-33` tells guardians without JavaScript to email support and says WAI-ME will "record your consent by email instead". No code in this slice implements that path with token validation, policy version, one-transaction activation, certificate issuance, or audit rows.
blockers.required_fix: Remove the promise to record consent by email, or implement a proper audited admin/manual consent path in scope with the same proof fields and lifecycle/certificate/audit guarantees.

blockers.title: Failed resend can corrupt expired-token state
blockers.evidence: `prepareGuardianSend` allows expired consent rows because it only refuses `confirmed` (`convex/guardians.ts:106-117`), then immediately patches `confirmation_state: "pending"` before Resend accepts the email (`convex/guardians.ts:154-160`). If Resend fails, `rollbackGuardianSend` restores only `confirmation_token_hash` and `token_sent_at`, not the previous `confirmation_state` (`convex/guardians.ts:196-210`). An expired row can therefore become persisted as pending after a failed send, violating AC2's expired-state requirement.
blockers.required_fix: Carry `prevState` through the prepare result and restore it on rollback, or defer changing `confirmation_state` until after a successful send while preserving token rotation safety.

blockers.title: Unsent resend attempts burn the minor's resend quota
blockers.evidence: For member-triggered resends, the per-member 1h/24h buckets are consumed before the global send budget is checked (`convex/guardians.ts:119-151`). Resend failures happen after `prepareGuardianSend` has committed those rate-limit writes, and rollback only restores the token (`convex/guardians.ts:251-276`). A global-cap refusal or Resend outage can therefore consume the member's limited self-serve resend attempts while sending no guardian email.
blockers.required_fix: Make refusal/failure rollback release or avoid member resend quota consumption for undelivered emails, or explicitly change the spec/tests to count failed attempts and show accurate wait-copy for each failure type.

non_blocking_notes: `npm test -- --run tests/unit/guardianToken.test.ts tests/convex/guardianConsent.test.ts` could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write `node_modules/.vite-temp/...`.
`npm run build` could not run in this read-only sandbox because Astro tried to write `.astro/content.d.ts`.
No committed secrets found by the targeted secret-pattern scan; no em dashes found in newly added member-facing guardian strings.

spec_compliance.criterion: AC1 guardian email sent at the right moment

spec_compliance.evidence: The button mutation exists, but the no-JS copy advertises an email consent bypass and failed resend can leave expired consent rows persisted as pending.

spec_compliance.criterion: AC3 confirmation activates member, certificate, audit
spec_compliance.evidence: `confirmGuardianConsent` patches consent proof, member age/lifecycle fields, writes two audit rows, and issues the certificate in one mutation (`convex/guardians.ts:415-449`).
Per-member throttles exist, but undelivered/global-refused resend attempts can still consume the member's resend quota.
Active `minor` members get the certificate plus AFG signpost branch and bypass adult tiles/settings (`src/portal/Dashboard.tsx:140-210`).
No em dash issue found, but the guardian-facing no-JS consent-by-email promise is not in the vault draft and changes the consent flow.
The no-JS page introduces an out-of-scope manual consent recording route.
Tests cover the main happy path and several resend cases, but they do not catch the expired-state rollback bug, quota burn on undelivered resend attempts, or the no-JS consent fallback promise.

summary: The core token-confirmation path is close, but this is a minors consent gate. The branch should not ship while it advertises an unaudited consent fallback and has resend rollback paths that can corrupt expiry state or strand a pending minor after no email was delivered.
